member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuBlendFactor
member.summary: Defines the multiplication weights that can be applied to either source color/alpha (new fragment) or destination color/alpha (existing render-target texel) during blending operations. Blending combines these colors/alpha values to achieve effects like transparency, anti-aliasing, or compositing.

remarks.para: The blending equation is Result = (SourceColor * SourceFactor) ◻ (DestinationColor * DestinationFactor) where ◻ is a blend operator (e.g., addition, subtraction). SourceFactor is the weight applied to the incoming fragment's color. DestinationFactor is the weight applied to the existing render-target color.

member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuCommandBuffer
member.summary: Represents a context for the CPU to queue up commands that are later all submitted at once to a instance for execution.

remarks.para: Commands only begin execution on the GPU once is called. Multiple instances have their commands executed relative to the order the instance is submitted. For example, if you submit command buffer A and then command buffer B, all commands in A will begin executing before any command in B begins executing.
instances are pooled and must not be used or referenced after calling . To get a instance call .
In multi-threading scenarios, you should only use and submit a instance on the thread you acquired it from.

It is valid to acquire multiple instances on the same thread at once. In fact a common design pattern is to acquire two command buffers per frame: one for render and compute passes and the other for copy passes and other preparatory work such as generating mipmaps. Interleaving commands between the two command buffers reduces the total amount of passes overall which improves rendering performance.

member.name: M:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuDevice.GetCommandBuffer
member.summary: Acquires a pooled instance associated with the device.

remarks.para: In multi-threading scenarios, calling is safe. However, the returned is not thread-safe; it must only be used and submitted on the thread it was acquired on.

It is valid to acquire multiple instances on the same thread at once. It's even a common design pattern to acquire two command buffers per frame: one for render and compute passes and the other for copy passes and other preparatory work such as generating mipmaps. Interleaving commands between the two command buffers reduces the total amount of passes overall which improves rendering performance.

member.returns: A pooled instance.

member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuGraphicsPipeline
member.summary: Represents the stages (steps) that a GPU performs in-order to convert 2D or 3D geometry into either: pixels that are displayed to the screen or texels written to an off-screen render-target.

remarks.para: Some stages are programmed using shaders, others have fixed or configurable behavior. The five main stages of the pipeline in-order include: the per-vertex shading stage, the rasterization stage, depth-test stage, stencil-test stage, and the per-fragment (per-pixel) shading stage. The per-vertex shading stage and per-fragment (per-pixel) shading stage are programmable. The rasterization stage, depth-test stage, and stencil-test stages are fixed but configurable. Remaining other stages are hidden from the perspective of the developer as they are fixed and non-configurable.
To create a , call with the specified struct.
To activate a for rendering, and by consequence deactivate any other active , call .

member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuGraphicsPipelineDepthStencilState
member.summary: Defines the configuration for the depth-test and stencil-test stages of the graphics pipeline.

remarks.para: The depth-test and stencil-test stages both test whether each fragment (pixel) should be kept or discarded for rendering before the per-fragment (per-pixel) shading stage in the graphics pipeline. Depth testing can be used to ensure correct occlusion of primitives (e.g. closer primitives hide farther ones). Stencil testing can be used for selective rendering of primitives' fragments (pixels).

Depth testing determines whether a fragment (pixel) is kept or discarded based on its depth (distance to the camera) to existing fragments' (pixels') depth values in the depth buffer (a.k.a. Z buffer). If the new fragment (pixel) is closer (passes the depth-test), the fragment (pixel) is kept and the depth buffer is updated. If the new fragment (pixel) is further away (fails the depth-test), the fragment (pixel) is discarded.

Stencil testing determines whether a fragment (pixel) is kept or discarded based on comparing its stencil value in the stencil buffer to a reference value using a specified boolean comparison operator (always true, always false, equal, greater than, etc). If the stencil-test passes for a fragment (pixel), the stencil buffer is updated with the reference value.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuGraphicsPipelineDepthStencilState.CompareOp
member.summary: Gets or sets the boolean comparison operator used for depth testing.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuGraphicsPipelineDepthStencilState.BackStencilState
member.summary: Gets or sets the stencil operation state for back-facing primitives.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuGraphicsPipelineDepthStencilState.FrontStencilState
member.summary: Gets or sets the stencil operation state for back-facing primitives.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuGraphicsPipelineDepthStencilState.ReadMask
member.summary: Gets or sets the bitmask that determines the bits of the stencil value that participate in the stencil test.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuGraphicsPipelineDepthStencilState.WriteMask
member.summary: Gets or sets the bitmask that determines the bits of the stencil value that are updated by the stencil test.

remarks.para: If is false, then is always false.

member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuGraphicsPipelineRasterizerState
member.summary: Defines the configuration for the rasterization stage of the graphics pipeline.

remarks.para: The rasterizer stage converts vertex primitives (points, lines, triangles) from the per-vertex stage into a raster image by performing scan-line conversion. During rasterization, vertices are transformed into the homogenous clip-space and clipped. As output, per-vertex attributes may be interpolated across the primitive (such as color) and made ready for the next stage of the graphics pipeline: depth-test stage, stencil-test stage, or per-fragment shading stage.

member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuGraphicsShader
member.summary: Represents a developer programmable graphics program used in the vertex stage or fragment stage of the graphics pipeline that transforms large amounts of data in parallel.

remarks.para: A vertex shader takes in vertex data (vertices) as input and transforms each vertex as output in parallel. Vertices represent points with attributes such as a position, a color, a normal vector, or texture coordinates (or other developer specified). These vertices form lines or triangles creating the building blocks for modeling any 2D or 3D geometry.

A fragment shader takes transformed information from the vertex shader and the hardware rasterization process as input and outputs a fragment: a color value for each rasterized pixel in parallel. When multi-sampling is enabled, the output is multiple fragments (pixels). When multi-sampling is disabled, the output is a single fragment (pixel).

member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSampler
member.summary: Represents a GPU resource that handles the mapping of texture elements (texels) of a into fragments (pixels) when rendering with a fragment (pixel) .

remarks.para: Texture sampling is the process of fetching and filtering texels (texture elements) from a texture map to determine the color or other attributes of a fragment (pixel) being rendered.
Texture fetching retrieves texel data from a texture map based on texture coordinates (UV or UVW coordinates) provided by the shader.
Texture filtering applies various techniques to determine the final color when texels don't align perfectly with the screen's or the render-target's pixels. See .

member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerAddressMode
member.summary: Defines how texture coordinates (UV coordinates or UVW coordinates) outside the [0, 1) range are handled when sampling.

member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerFilterMode
member.summary: Defines the different strategies for how texels of a are mapped into fragments (pixels), also known as sampling. Depending on the strategy chosen the pixels will show a varying degree of blurriness, detail, and aliasing.

remarks.para:
There are 3 different cases for filtering.
Each texel maps onto more than one fragment (pixel). This is known as magnification.
Each texel maps exactly onto one fragment (pixel). Filtering does not apply in this case.
Each texel maps onto less than one fragment (pixel). This is known as minification.
Magnification and minification can be set for a in the when calling .

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerFilterMode.Nearest
member.summary: Uses the closest texel (in Manhattan distance) to the center of fragment (pixel) being rendered.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerFilterMode.Linear
member.summary: Interpolates between the four nearest texels (in Manhattan distance) to the center of the fragment (pixel) being rendered.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.MagnificationFilterMode
member.summary: Gets or sets the used in sampling when each texel maps onto more than one fragment (pixel). Default is .

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.MinificationFilterMode
member.summary: Gets or sets the used in sampling when each texel maps onto less than one fragment (pixel). Default is .

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.MipMapMode
member.summary: Gets or sets the used in sampling. Default is .

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.AddressModeU
member.summary: Gets or sets the used in sampling when the texture width coordinate (U) is outside [0, 1). Default is .

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.AddressModeV
member.summary: Gets or sets the used in sampling when the texture height coordinate (V) is outside [0, 1). Default is .

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.AddressModeW
member.summary: Gets or sets the used in sampling when the texture depth coordinate (W) is outside [0, 1). Default is .

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.MaximumAnisotropy
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of texels to sample per fragment (pixel) when the viewing angle of a texture is at a sharp angle. For best results, use with mipmapping. Must be between 1 and 16, inclusively. Default is 1. If is false, is ignored.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.MinimumLevelOfDetailClamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the lower end of the mipmap range to clamp access to where 0 represents the largest and most detailed mipmap and any higher level is less detailed. Default is 0.0f.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.MaximumLevelOfDetailClamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the higher end of the mipmap range to clamp access to where 0 represents the largest and most detailed mipmap and any higher level is less detailed. Must be greater than or equal to . Default is .

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.IsEnabledAnisotropy
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether anisotropic filtering is enabled or disabled. Default is false.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.IsEnabledDepthCompare
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether depth sampling and comparison against a reference value is enabled or disabled. Default is false.

member.name: P:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuSamplerOptions.DepthCompareOp
member.summary: Gets or sets the used for comparing a sampled depth value from the depth texture against a reference value when is true. If is false, is ignored.

member.name: T:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuShaderFormats
member.summary: Defines the data formats (byte code or source code) available for graphics or compute shaders.

remarks.para: Each format corresponds to a specific that accepts it.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuShaderFormats.None
member.summary: Shader data format is not specified. Defaults to the available shader formats for the current operating system.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuShaderFormats.Private
member.summary: Shader data format is specific to a platform/driver behind a non-disclosure agreement (NDA).

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuShaderFormats.SPIRV
member.summary: Shader data format is binary pre-compiled Standard Portable Intermediate Representation - Vulkan (SPIR-V). This format is specific to .

remarks.para: usually has the file extension '.spv'.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuShaderFormats.DXBC
member.summary: Shader data format is binary pre-compiled DirectX 12 Byte Code (DXBC) using Shader Model 5.1 (SM5_1). This format is specific to .

remarks.para: usually has the file extension '.dxbc'.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuShaderFormats.DXIL
member.summary: Shader data format is binary pre-compiled DirectX 12 Intermediate Language (DXIL) using Shader Model 6.0 (SM6_0). This format is specific to .

remarks.para: usually has the file extension '.dxil'.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuShaderFormats.MSL
member.summary: Shader data format is text Metal Shader Language (MSL). This format is specific to .

remarks.para: usually has the file extension '.msl'.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.GpuShaderFormats.MetalLib
member.summary: Shader data format is binary pre-compiled Metal Library. This format is specific to .

remarks.para: usually has the file extension '.metallib'.

member.name: M:bottlenoselabs.SDL.Renderer2D.Clear
member.summary: Clears the current entire rendering target with the ignoring the viewport and the clip rectangle.

member.name: M:bottlenoselabs.SDL.Renderer2D.Present
member.summary: Updates the screen with any rendering performed.

remarks.para: Call once a frame to present the back buffer to the screen. Rendering is always performed on the back buffer first.

The back buffer should be considered invalidated after calling . It is strongly encouraged to call to initialize the back buffer before any rendering operations even if you plan to overwrite every pixel.
When rendering to a , there is no need to call and it should not be called because textures don't have a back buffer. Calling is for only updating the screen.

remarks.para: When vertical synchronization is enabled, it forces the GPU to wait for the monitor's refresh cycle (measured in hertz or seconds per cycle) before rendering a new frame which can help prevent possible screen tearing at the cost of possible input lag. Screen tearing is misaligned frames when the GPU outputs rendered frames faster than the monitor can display them causing possible unwanted visual artifacts.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.VSyncMode.EnabledEveryRefresh
member.summary: Vertical synchronization is enabled for every vertical refresh.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.VSyncMode.EnabledEverySecondRefresh
member.summary: Vertical synchronization is enabled for every second vertical refresh.

member.name: F:bottlenoselabs.SDL.VSyncMode.Adaptive
member.summary: Vertical synchronization flips between enabled or disabled depending on the frame rate.
